summary: Iteration 7 backend + frontend testing. All 11 backend pytest cases PASS (auth, rooms code visibility, room_visits code-appearance, notifications /me + read + read-all, events RBAC, grant-host notification+event, WebSocket knock flow both admit + deny). Frontend mostly works, but a CRITICAL frontend gap was found: viewer Dashboard does NOT honor backend's `code=null` — share-block and 'Enter room' text render unconditionally.

ui_bugs.issue: Share-block (code + link) is rendered UNCONDITIONALLY for every room. It is NOT wrapped in `{r.code && (...)}`. As a result, a fresh viewer who has never joined a room STILL sees the room code and link on every card, using `r.room_id.toUpperCase()` directly. This breaks the code-visibility requirement — backend returns `code=null` for viewers but the UI ignores it.

The join-room button text is always 'Enter room'. The spec requires 'Knock to join' when `r.code` is null (i.e. the user hasn't joined the room yet). Current code has no conditional based on `r.code`.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.js (lines ~243-285), wrap the share-block <div> in `{r.code && (...)}` so it only renders when the backend exposes the code.
In the same file, change the join-room button label to depend on `r.code`: `{r.code ? 'Enter room' : 'Knock to join'}` (or similar wording) so viewers see they need to knock.
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.js currently reads `r.room_id.toUpperCase()` inside share-block instead of `r.code` — even after the code-visibility fix, prefer reading `r.code` so the truth source is the backend field.
server.py file is ~1189 lines — consider splitting into modules (auth.py, rooms.py, ws.py, notifications.py, events.py) for maintainability.
Two identical copy-code handlers on same button pair; consider extracting a single wrapper.
The `/api/notifications` (super-admin broadcast list) endpoint co-exists with `/api/notifications/me`; consider deprecating the older one or renaming for clarity.
test_credentials: sidroks@hotmail.com / streamstar@1 (super admin). Freshly-registered viewer accounts require email_verified=true — force-verify via Mongo since RESEND is configured but preview inbox is unreachable.
seed_data_creation: Test viewer accounts (email prefix `test_knock_*`, `test_ui_v_*`) created via /api/auth/register + force-verified in Mongo. One host_granted event was logged for the test viewer during grant-host test (then revoked).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend behavior fully green: knock flow, notifications, events, code visibility, rename all working. The only remaining bug is in Dashboard.js — the two conditional-rendering fixes described in action_items. Once fixed, retest the viewer flow: fresh viewer login -> share-block absent -> button labeled 'Knock to join' -> clicking navigates to /watch/<id> -> knock-waiting screen (which already works).
rca of the issue:
Root cause: Dashboard.js was updated to add data-testids for share-block/copy-code/copy-room but the conditional wrapping `{r.code && ...}` and button text switch based on `r.code` presence, which were part of the iteration-7 spec, were never applied. Backend correctly returns `code: null` for non-visited rooms — the frontend just doesn't consume the flag. Reproduction: (1) POST /api/auth/register a new viewer, force-verify email in Mongo, login. (2) Load /dashboard. (3) Every room card shows the purple share-block with the code (uppercased room_id) and link. Expected: no share-block for un-visited rooms. Mitigation: change lines 244-285 to `{r.code && (
...{r.code.toUpperCase()}...
)}` and line 289 button text to `{r.code ? 'Enter room' : 'Knock to join'}`.
